description: Goal: let jobs state whether they need current evidence for iteration or settled evidence for safe downstream consumption. Signal: scheduler tests prove a dirty present fact satisfies current readers but blocks settled waiters, stable recompute can wake settled waiters without a revision bump, and multi-publisher facts settle only when every relevant producer is clean. Strategy: replace raw read/wait fact sets with FactUse keyed by readiness, track content-dirty jobs separately from present facts, compute settledness from producer cleanliness and the read dependency cone, and wake on readiness transitions as well as revision transitions.
title: Compiler2 fact engine: make current and settled fact uses first-class
